puzzle.title: The Grief of a Patient Soul
puzzle.intro: A profound sorrow tested this soul, a sorrow so deep it threatened to steal their sight. Yet, through unwavering patience and trust in Allah's plan, a scent carried on the wind brought news of a reunion long prayed for. Can you identify this figure whose faith never faltered, even in the face of immense grief?
clues: I was blessed with prophethood, following in the footsteps of my esteemed forebears.
My other name, by which a nation would later be called, is Isra'il.
A dream of celestial bodies bowing down was shared with me, and I advised that it be kept a secret from my other children.
I was consumed by such sorrow over the loss of a beloved child that my eyes turned white from grief.
Years later, I hesitated to send my youngest child on a journey with his siblings, taking a solemn promise from them for his safe return.
My sight was miraculously restored when the garment of my long-lost son was cast over my face, carrying his scent from the land of Egypt.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yusuf (Joseph)
Prophet Ishaq (Isaac)
Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob)
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Zakariyya

trial.label: What Was My Primary Trial?
options: Being swallowed by a great fish
Building an ark to survive a great flood
The prolonged separation from a beloved son
Confronting a tyrannical king who claimed divinity
Suffering from a severe, prolonged illness
trial.answer: The prolonged separation from a beloved son

location.label: What Key Object Brought Me Relief?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A clay bird brought to life
A she-camel that emerged from a rock
A son's garment (shirt)
A ring that controlled jinn and animals

outcome.label: What Was The Ultimate Outcome?
options: My people were destroyed by a mighty wind
I led my people across a parted sea to escape a tyrant
Reunion with my family and prostration in fulfillment of a dream
My wife was turned into a pillar of salt for looking back
The fire I was thrown into became cool and peaceful
outcome.answer: Reunion with my family and prostration in fulfillment of a dream

puzzle.arabic: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ نَافِلَةًۭ ۖ وَكُلًّۭا جَعَلْنَا صَـٰلِحِينَ
puzzle.verse: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous. (21:72)
